title: jarilla caudata
intro: Jarilla caudata, also known as Jarilla or Desert Lavender, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a shrub with fragrant purple flowers and has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including relieving respiratory issues, reducing stress, and promoting skin health, due to its high levels of essential oils and flavonoids. Jarilla caudata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and topical creams, and is also used in traditional folk medicine for its therapeutic properties.
intro_study: According to "Phytotherapy research : PTR", jarilla caudata, which is a part of the Malvaceae family, has been reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and wound healing activities, strong antioxidant properties, and antimicrobial effects.
